items.title: This site looks like it was built by a team of 3D developers It wasn't. It's just video. And tha
items.description: This site looks like it was built by a team of 3D developers It wasn't. It's just video. And that's the part nobody expects > he generated two frames with AI > turned them into a clip with Seedance 2.0 > then gave it to
items.prompt: This site looks like it was built by a team of 3D developers It wasn't. It's just video. And that's the part nobody expects > he generated two frames with AI > turned them into a clip with Seedance 2.0 > then gave it to Claude 4.8 to wrap into a full immersive scrollable experience Three effects that make the user feel like they're physically moving through a space: > see-through scrub > 3D scroll > mouse tracking None of them are actually 3D. The browser never renders a single polygon. It's a video being controlled by scroll position and cursor movement Most people assume sites like this require a creative developer who specializes in WebGL and weeks of optimization. The actual pipeline is three steps and fits in an afternoon full walkthrough in the article below. bookmark this before you need it

items.title: iPhone camera control for Seedance 2.0 the app for 3D camera and AI motion transfer is now in te
items.description: iPhone camera control for Seedance 2.0 the app for 3D camera and AI motion transfer is now in test flight, link below. You stage the scene and can walk around it and capture real camera movement in 3D space, then Seedanc
items.prompt: iPhone camera control for Seedance 2.0 the app for 3D camera and AI motion transfer is now in test flight, link below. You stage the scene and can walk around it and capture real camera movement in 3D space, then Seedance brings the scene to life. Handheld camera motion and realistic shake is hard to describe or prompt to Al. The iphone's ARKit is super precise with motion tracking and you can record complex scenes as a one man studio. The characters can move and I am working on motion capture. Here is the Al filmmaking workflow: 1. Set the scene in 3D space 2. Record camera motion 3. Generate a starting image 4. Feed image and video to Seedance This was a quick build with claude code but now it is in test flight. Happy if it helps others.

You stage the scene and can walk around it and capture real camera movement in 3D space, then Seedance brings the scene to life. Handheld camera motion and realistic shake is hard to describe or prompt to Al. The iphone's ARKit is super precise with motion tracking and you can record complex scenes as a one man studio. The characters can move and I am working on motion capture. Here is the Al filmmaking workflow: 1. Set the scene in 3D space 2. Record camera motion 3. Generate a starting image 4. Feed image and video to Seedance This was a quick build with claude code but now it is in test flight. Happy if it helps others.

items.title: On this day in 2023, Elon Musk officially announced xAI. A lot has happened since then… • Jul 12
items.description: On this day in 2023, Elon Musk officially announced xAI. A lot has happened since then… • Jul 12, 2023 — xAI was officially announced with the mission of understanding the true nature of the universe. • Nov 3, 2023 — Unv
items.prompt: On this day in 2023, Elon Musk officially announced xAI. A lot has happened since then… • Jul 12, 2023 — xAI was officially announced with the mission of understanding the true nature of the universe. • Nov 3, 2023 — Unveiled Grok, its first AI model, trained in only two months and connected to real-time knowledge from 𝕏. • Nov 6, 2023 — Launched PromptIDE for prompt engineering and interpretability research. • Mar 17, 2024 — Open-released Grok-1’s weights and architecture, a 314B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model. • Mar 28, 2024 — Announced Grok-1.5 with major reasoning improvements and a 128K-token context window. • Apr 12, 2024 — Introduced Grok-1.5V, xAI’s first multimodal model for understanding images, documents, charts, diagrams and screenshots. • May 26, 2024 — Raised $6 billion in Series B funding. • Aug 13, 2024 — Launched Grok-2 and Grok-2 mini, bringing stronger reasoning, coding, vision and image generation to Grok. • Sep 2024 — Brought Colossus online with 100,000 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, built from start to finish in only 122 days. • Nov 4, 2024 — Opened the xAI API public beta. • Dec 9, 2024 — Launched Aurora, xAI’s own autoregressive image-generation model. • Dec 12, 2024 — Made Grok free for everyone on 𝕏 while adding web search, citations and multilingual improvements. • Dec 23, 2024 — Raised another $6 billion through its Series C funding round. • Jan–Feb 2025 — Launched standalone Grok applications for iOS and Android. • Feb 2025 — Doubled Colossus to 200,000 GPUs just 92 days after completing the first 100,000-GPU cluster. • Feb 19, 2025 — Unveiled Grok 3 and Grok 3 mini, trained with roughly 10× more compute, alongside Think and DeepSearch. • Feb–Mar 2025 — Rolled out Voice Mode and the SuperGrok subscription. • Mar 17, 2025 — Acquired AI video-generation startup Hotshot. • Mar 28, 2025 — xAI acquired 𝕏 in an all-stock transaction, combining data, models, compute, distribution and talent. • Apr–May 2025 — Released Grok 3 through the API and launched Live Search for real-time information from the web and 𝕏. • Jul 9, 2025 — Launched Grok 4 and Grok 4 Heavy with native tool use, real-time search and reinforcement learning at unprecedented scale. • Jul 12, 2025 — Brought Grok directly into Tesla vehicles through an over-the-air software update. • Jul 14, 2025 — Launched xAI for Government. • Jul–Aug 2025 — Introduced Grok Companions, launched Grok Imagine for rapid image and video generation with sound, and open-released Grok 2. • Aug 28, 2025 — Launched Grok Code Fast 1 for fast and economical agentic coding. • Sep 19, 2025 — Launched Grok 4 Fast, delivering Grok 4-class performance with significantly greater token and cost efficiency. • Sep 25, 2025 — Expanded xAI for Government through the GSA OneGov program. • Oct 27, 2025 — Launched Grokipedia, an AI-generated knowledge platform powered by Grok. • Nov 17, 2025 — Launched Grok 4.1, which reached the #1 position on LMArena’s Text Arena during its rollout. • Nov 19, 2025 — Launched Grok 4.1 Fast and the Agent Tools API while announcing a major partnership with Saudi Arabia and HUMAIN. • Dec 2025 — Partnered with El Salvador on a nationwide AI education program; launched the Grok Voice Agent API, Collections API, Grok Business and Grok Enterprise; and was selected for a major U.S. defense AI program. • By the end of 2025 — Colossus I and II had surpassed one million H100 GPU equivalents, while 𝕏 and Grok reached roughly 600 million monthly active users combined. • Jan 6, 2026 — Raised $20 billion through an upsized Series E funding round. • Jan 28, 2026 — Launched the Grok Imagine API for image creation, video generation and media editing. • Feb 2, 2026 — SpaceX acquired xAI, combining artificial intelligence, rockets, satellites, communications and real-time information. • Mar 10, 2026 — Launched Grok 4.20 and Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent, allowing multiple specialized agents to collaborate on difficult problems. • Apr 17–30, 2026 — Launched standalone Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs, Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 and Custom Voices. • May 5, 2026 — Launched Grok 4.3 with a 1M-token context window, configurable reasoning and advanced tool-calling capabilities. • May 6, 2026 — Launched Grok Connectors and Imagine Quality Mode while announcing a major Colossus computing partnership with Anthropic. • May 15–21, 2026 — Brought Grok subscriptions to Hermes Agent, OpenClaw and OpenCode, then launched Grok Skills for persistent expertise, workflow automation and document generation. • May 25, 2026 — Launched Grok Build, an agentic coding CLI capable of planning, searching, editing, testing and building software directly from the terminal. • May 27–Jun 15, 2026 — Expanded the Grok coding ecosystem with Kilo Code, Grok Build through the API, Composer 2.5, plugins, a marketplace, an Agent Dashboard and Warp integration. • Jun 3–18, 2026 — Launched Grok Imagine Video 1.5, expanded Grok Voice through Vapi, powered Gopuff’s Go and eToro’s Tori, and brought Grok to PowerPoint, Word, Excel, Amazon Bedrock and Databricks. • Jul 1, 2026 — Launched Voice Agent Builder, a no-code platform for deploying production-ready Grok voice agents. • Jul 6, 2026 — Launched 21 new flagship Grok voices. • Jul 8, 2026 — Launched Grok 4.5, SpaceXAI’s smartest model yet for coding, agentic tasks and knowledge work—trained alongside Cursor, served at fast-model speeds and built for dramatically greater token efficiency.

items.title: Claude Fable running ComfyUI workflows through the Comfy MCP is a cheat code. → Claude pulled th
items.description: Claude Fable running ComfyUI workflows through the Comfy MCP is a cheat code. → Claude pulled the shot from the web, auto-detected the scene cuts + trimmed it → ran my saved depthanything v3 + openpose workflow to build
items.prompt: Claude Fable running ComfyUI workflows through the Comfy MCP is a cheat code. → Claude pulled the shot from the web, auto-detected the scene cuts + trimmed it → ran my saved depthanything v3 + openpose workflow to build the driving reference video → scrubbed every frame for the sharpest one, then character swapped me in with gpt-image-2 exactly how I directed it: wearing a tuxedo, liminal space environment, no background characters → wrote the seedance 2.0 prompts + ran the gens → stitched together the 3-panel comparison you're watching The preprocessing + prompt writing is usually most of the work on a clip like this. With the MCP I didn't touch any of it. Didn't need to look at a single node lol.

items.title: Do you realize what the hell just happened? Fable 5 just flipped the entire website-building gam
items.description: Do you realize what the hell just happened? Fable 5 just flipped the entire website-building game right in front of you. Fully animated, studio-looking sites, smooth scrolling, 3D globes, video backgrounds, all mobile re
items.prompt: Do you realize what the hell just happened? Fable 5 just flipped the entire website-building game right in front of you. Fully animated, studio-looking sites, smooth scrolling, 3D globes, video backgrounds, all mobile responsive, built in one session by describing what you want. No agency, no code, no months of work. Here's the workflow, start to finish: 1. Find a look on Pinterest. Grab an image with the vibe you want (3D, animation, depth). 2. Turn it into a video. Use an AI image-to-video tool (Higgsfield, Kling) to animate it. Tell it to move but not zoom or reposition. 3. Build the hero with Fable 5. In Claude Code, describe the hero section, drop your video in as the background, set your colors. 4. Build section by section. Ask for the next sections in the same style. Once Fable has your fonts, colors, and layout locked, it reuses them and avoids generic AI-slop pages. 5. Refine with references. Screenshot a background, blur it in Figma to grab the average color, and tell Fable to match it. 6. Post it to grow. Screen-record the site, post the clip, and tag the tools you used. Companies repost work that shows off their platform. The people building real things with Fable now are going to be way ahead when everyone else catches up. Bookmark this.

items.title: Cooking meets cinematic AI. This stunning commercial was created with Seedance 2.0 in @LartAI ..
items.description: Cooking meets cinematic AI. This stunning commercial was created with Seedance 2.0 in @Lart_AI Delivering realistic motion, rich food details, and premium visuals that look like a real production. Create yours here 👇  P
items.prompt: Cooking meets cinematic AI. This stunning commercial was created with Seedance 2.0 in @Lart_AI Delivering realistic motion, rich food details, and premium visuals that look like a real production. Create yours here 👇  Prompt: THE FLAME CHEF - 15 second high-energy cinematic cooking film, 10 rapid scenes, fast rhythmic pacing, whip-pan and speed-ramp transitions between every scene, kinetic kitchen artistry from fresh ingredients to final plated dish. Shot on ARRI Alexa 35, 35mm lens, natural film grain, rich warm color grade with deep shadows and golden highlights, professional food cinematography, photorealistic, not CGI. Dark moody restaurant kitchen, dramatic overhead lighting, steam and warm glow. SCENE 1 (0-1.5s): Chef's hand places a cast iron pan onto a glowing gas burner with confident energy, blue flame rising around the edges, quick real time then micro slow motion on the flame glow, hard whip-pan into locked close-up. SCENE 2 (1.5-3s): Rapid skilled vegetable prep, knife rocking smoothly through fresh herbs and bell peppers in rhythmic motion, hyper-fast real time with hands in controlled motion, one pepper slice spinning in brief slow motion, top-down locked shot. SCENE 3 (3-4.5s): Butter cube tossed gracefully through the air landing in the warm pan, melting instantly into golden foam and swirl, slow motion flight with speed ramp to real time on the sizzle, side macro tracking the arc. SCENE 4 (4.5-6s): Chef flips the pan, vegetables rising upward in a golden arc, warm flame glow beneath, tiny seasoning embers drifting, speed ramp from real time flip into dramatic slow motion at the peak of the toss, low hero angle. SCENE 5 (6-7.5s): Extreme macro, garlic and chili meeting hot oil, lively golden bubbles, steam rising in a soft backlit plume, 120fps slow motion with every bubble crisp, probe lens push-in through the steam. SCENE 6 (7.5-9s): Chef seasoning from height with flowing hand movement, salt crystals raining down in slow motion through a shaft of overhead light catching like snow, slow motion rain against fast hand movement, side close-up with shallow focus on the crystals. SCENE 7 (9-10.5s): Sauce poured from a steel pan in a glossy ribbon coating the dish in one continuous silky wave, steam curling, silky hypnotic slow motion, orbiting macro around the pour. SCENE 8 (10.5-12s): Fresh herbs dropped from above, leaves tumbling in slow motion landing perfectly on the glistening dish, tiny sparkle of sauce, slow motion fall with real time landing, top-down locked shot. SCENE 9 (12-13.5s): Chef wipes the plate rim in one confident swift motion and spins the plate a quarter turn, steam rising through dramatic side light, fast confident real time, close-up tracking the hand with whip-pan on the spin. SCENE 10 (13.5-15s): Final hero shot, finished dish center frame under a single overhead spotlight, steam rising in elegant curls, background fading to darkness, chef's silhouette stepping back with satisfaction, calm slow motion after the energy, slow push-in settling to a loop-friendly hold. Movement: continuous kinetic energy throughout, hands moving with professional speed and precision, constant speed ramping between hyper-fast real time and crisp slow motion for rhythm, ingredients gracefully in flight, intensity building from scene 1 to 9 then sudden calm hero ending Camera: hard whip-pans between scenes, top-down locked shots, side macro tracking, low hero angle, probe lens push-in, orbiting pour shot, close-up hand tracking, slow push-in finale, all cuts punchy and landing on beat Effects: warm flame glow, backlit steam plumes, golden oil bubbles in extreme macro, salt crystals in overhead light shafts, glossy sauce reflections, shallow depth of field, natural film grain, dark moody kitchen atmosphere, no text no watermark.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 Walking Home in the Rain, created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject: Young Ko
items.description: Seedance 2.0 Walking Home in the Rain, created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject: Young Korean woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black wavy hair loosely tied into a slightly messy ponytail with wispy bangs. Lig
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 Walking Home in the Rain, created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject: Young Korean woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black wavy hair loosely tied into a slightly messy ponytail with wispy bangs. Light grey zip hoodie worn open over a faded white tank top. Relaxed blue jeans. Black canvas sneakers. Natural skin texture, minimal makeup, ordinary everyday look. Consistent appearance throughout the video. Location: Authentic Korean residential neighborhood during a light summer rain shower. Narrow concrete alleys, low-rise houses, small gardens, potted plants, bicycles, utility poles, overhead cables, wet pavement, puddles, rain-darkened walls, leafy trees. Camera Style Early-2000s consumer DV camcorder. Friend casually filming. Heavy handheld shake. Imperfect framing. Subject frequently drifts toward frame edges. Late reframing. Autofocus hunting. Lens breathing. Exposure pumping. Motion blur. Rolling shutter. Digital compression artifacts. Slight sensor noise. Soft detail. Faded colors. Low contrast. Occasional blown highlights. No stabilization. No cinematic movements. No modern color grading. The camera operator behaves like a real friend with no filmmaking experience. 00:00–00:02 Standing beneath a small apartment awning while rain falls across the street. She leans forward and extends her hand into the rain to test how heavy it is. A passing vehicle somewhere off-frame splashes water nearby. She immediately laughs and pulls her hand back. The camera misses part of the reaction because the operator reframes too slowly. Rain droplets briefly appear on the lens. Autofocus hunts between her face and the rainy street. 00:02–00:04 She steps into the drizzle and starts walking down the narrow alley. The camera follows unevenly from behind. Her sneakers splash through shallow puddles. Wet strands of hair begin sticking to her cheek and neck. Wind moves her hoodie slightly. The operator almost loses her from frame while turning a corner. 00:04–00:06 Passing a row of potted plants outside a house. She casually brushes rainwater from a large leaf. The camera unexpectedly focuses on the leaf instead of her face. Exposure shifts as the scene moves from open sky into tree shade. She continues walking before camera catches up. 00:06–00:08 A neighborhood cat shelters beneath a parked bicycle. She crouches down briefly and says a few friendly words. The cat sniffs her hand. Camera focus repeatedly jumps between the bicycle wheel, the cat, and her face. One moment is partially blocked by the operator's movement. 00:08–00:10 Walking again. Rainwater drips from overhead branches onto her shoulder. She reacts naturally and laughs to herself. The camera is slightly too close and awkwardly reframes. A gust of wind moves nearby leaves and loose hair strands. 00:10–00:12 Near a small intersection. She pauses while waiting for a scooter to pass in the distance. The camera drifts toward the wet road before finding her again. She wipes rain from her forehead using her sleeve. Her clothing shows subtle dampness from the weather. 00:12–00:15 Walking away down the rain-darkened street. The camera trails several steps behind. She suddenly realizes she is still being filmed. She glances back over her shoulder. A small genuine smile appears. She looks away again and keeps walking. The operator lowers the camcorder slightly. Recording abruptly cuts to black mid-motion as if the camera was accidentally switched off. Audio (Natural only) Light rainfall. Water dripping from roofs and leaves. Footsteps on wet concrete. Distant neighborhood traffic. Occasional scooter passing far away. Birds sheltering in trees. Rustling leaves. Faint residential chatter. Cat sounds. Subtle wind. No music. No narration. No sound design. No added effects. Generation Priority Authenticity > Beauty Believability > Cinematics Natural Imperfections > Technical Perfection The final result should look indistinguishable from a real early-2000s camcorder scene.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footag
items.description: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footage vacation video, 15 seconds, seven shots with hard cuts — a tourist's camera roll from one full day, morning to dusk. Image
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footage vacation video, 15 seconds, seven shots with hard cuts — a tourist's camera roll from one full day, morning to dusk. Image reference: Use the attached gate photo as the exact visual reference for Shot 1 — match its architecture, proportions, torch placement, lighting, and signage precisely. Main subject: Young woman, blond hair in a loose low bun shedding strands, oversized faded olive t-shirt, denim shorts, white canvas sneakers, disposable-camera strap on her wrist. Realistic skin texture, minimal makeup, wide-eyed tourist joy. Maintain perfect visual consistency — identity, hair, clothing, proportions — across every shot and location. Location: A vast island dinosaur preserve. Jungle mountains, open savanna paddocks, wooden observation structures, tall electrified perimeter fences, dirt tour roads. Dinosaurs are photorealistic animals — muted natural hides, birdlike motion, visible breathing weight. Filmed like wildlife, never like monsters. Camera style: Mid-range smartphone, handheld by her friend. Heavy shake, autofocus hunting, motion blur on pans, compression grain, exposure pumping. Each cut is abrupt and mid-motion, like real unedited camera-roll clips. 00:00–00:03 — THE GATES (morning, filmed from inside a moving open-top tour jeep). The camera films forward over the seats as the jeep approaches the colossal timber gates from the reference image — flared stone-and-wood towers, flaming torches, jungle pressing in on both sides, the road still wet from morning rain. The doors groan open. She spins around from the front seat into frame, gripping the roll bar, and half-shouts over the engine: "Oh my god, this is wild! I can't believe we're actually here!" — as the jeep passes under the archway into shadow and out again. 00:03–00:05 — SAVANNA (late morning, wide shot from a raised wooden platform). She stands small at the rail against an open plain where a herd of long-necked giants wades through heat haze, one rearing slowly against the treeline. The camera zooms in shakily, overshoots, corrects. 00:05–00:07 — RIVER CROSSING (midday, low angle through the jeep's open side). Past her shoulder as the jeep fords a shallow river: a crested duck-billed dinosaur drinks twenty meters away, lifting its head to watch them pass, water dripping from its jaw. She raises her disposable camera; the phone catches its tiny click. 00:07–00:09 — JUNGLE FENCE (afternoon, handheld walking shot). Dim under the canopy beside a tall humming fence. She peers through the wire — and a small crested predator peers back from the ferns, head tilting in quick birdlike jerks. She backs up a slow half-step, whispering "okay... hi." Autofocus argues between wire and eye. 00:09–00:11 — THE HERD RUNS (late afternoon, chaotic pan from the jeep at speed). A flock of small striped dinosaurs floods across and around the vehicle. The camera whips left-right, blurred bodies streaking past, her whooping off-mic, dust on the lens. 00:11–00:13 — QUIET GIANT (golden hour, static shot at a wooden overlook). The frame finally settles: silhouetted against low sun, she stands at a rail as an immense horned dinosaur grazes just below, close enough to hear grass tearing. She isn't laughing anymore — just watching. 00:13–00:15 — DUSK ROAR (blue hour, half-framed and accidental). Filmed as the phone was being lowered, frame tilted: a colossal, deep bellow rolls in from the darkening jungle far off. Everyone freezes and turns toward it. Cut to black mid-turn. Audio: Ambient only, shifting per location — jeep engine and gate groan, her excited line over the motor, savanna wind, river wading, fence hum, thundering small feet, grass tearing, and the single distant bellow that swallows everything. No music. No narration. Style & quality boosters: Raw consumer camera-roll aesthetic, abrupt mid-motion cuts, heavy handheld instability, natural motion blur, golden-hour lens flare, coherent animal mass and physics — ground tremor, water displacement, dust. No text overlays, no watermarks. Goal: One unforgettable day inside the world's most famous dinosaur preserve, told through seven imperfect tourist clips — opening on the gates everyone recognizes, and closing on the sound that reminds them what lives beyond the fences.

items.rawText: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footage vacation video, 15 seconds, seven shots with hard cuts — a tourist's camera roll from one full day, morning to dusk. Image reference: Use the attached gate photo as the exact visual reference for Shot 1 — match its architecture, proportions, torch placement, lighting, and signage precisely. Main subject: Young woman, blond hair in a loose low bun shedding strands, oversized faded olive t-shirt, denim shorts, white canvas sneakers, disposable-camera strap on her wrist. Realistic skin texture, minimal makeup, wide-eyed tourist joy. Maintain perfect visual consistency — identity, hair, clothing, proportions — across every shot and location. Location: A vast island dinosaur preserve. Jungle mountains, open savanna paddocks, wooden observation structures, tall electrified perimeter fences, dirt tour roads. Dinosaurs are photorealistic animals — muted natural hides, birdlike motion, visible breathing weight. Filmed like wildlife, never like monsters. Camera style: Mid-range smartphone, handheld by her friend. Heavy shake, autofocus hunting, motion blur on pans, compression grain, exposure pumping. Each cut is abrupt and mid-motion, like real unedited camera-roll clips. 00:00–00:03 — THE GATES (morning, filmed from inside a moving open-top tour jeep). The camera films forward over the seats as the jeep approaches the colossal timber gates from the reference image — flared stone-and-wood towers, flaming torches, jungle pressing in on both sides, the road still wet from morning rain. The doors groan open. She spins around from the front seat into frame, gripping the roll bar, and half-shouts over the engine: "Oh my god, this is wild! I can't believe we're actually here!" — as the jeep passes under the archway into shadow and out again. 00:03–00:05 — SAVANNA (late morning, wide shot from a raised wooden platform). She stands small at the rail against an open plain where a herd of long-necked giants wades through heat haze, one rearing slowly against the treeline. The camera zooms in shakily, overshoots, corrects. 00:05–00:07 — RIVER CROSSING (midday, low angle through the jeep's open side). Past her shoulder as the jeep fords a shallow river: a crested duck-billed dinosaur drinks twenty meters away, lifting its head to watch them pass, water dripping from its jaw. She raises her disposable camera; the phone catches its tiny click. 00:07–00:09 — JUNGLE FENCE (afternoon, handheld walking shot). Dim under the canopy beside a tall humming fence. She peers through the wire — and a small crested predator peers back from the ferns, head tilting in quick birdlike jerks. She backs up a slow half-step, whispering "okay... hi." Autofocus argues between wire and eye. 00:09–00:11 — THE HERD RUNS (late afternoon, chaotic pan from the jeep at speed). A flock of small striped dinosaurs floods across and around the vehicle. The camera whips left-right, blurred bodies streaking past, her whooping off-mic, dust on the lens. 00:11–00:13 — QUIET GIANT (golden hour, static shot at a wooden overlook). The frame finally settles: silhouetted against low sun, she stands at a rail as an immense horned dinosaur grazes just below, close enough to hear grass tearing. She isn't laughing anymore — just watching. 00:13–00:15 — DUSK ROAR (blue hour, half-framed and accidental). Filmed as the phone was being lowered, frame tilted: a colossal, deep bellow rolls in from the darkening jungle far off. Everyone freezes and turns toward it. Cut to black mid-turn. Audio: Ambient only, shifting per location — jeep engine and gate groan, her excited line over the motor, savanna wind, river wading, fence hum, thundering small feet, grass tearing, and the single distant bellow that swallows everything. No music. No narration. Style & quality boosters: Raw consumer camera-roll aesthetic, abrupt mid-motion cuts, heavy handheld instability, natural motion blur, golden-hour lens flare, coherent animal mass and physics — ground tremor, water displacement, dust. No text overlays, no watermarks. Goal: One unforgettable day inside the world's most famous dinosaur preserve, told through seven imperfect tourist clips — opening on the gates everyone recognizes, and closing on the sound that reminds them what lives beyond the fences.

items.title: POV: The kiss that broke the internet, recreated with AI on the tip of Tokyo Tower. A viral cine
items.description: POV: The kiss that broke the internet, recreated with AI on the tip of Tokyo Tower. A viral cinematic moment, reimagined with seamless camera movement, perfect character consistency, and a breathtaking skyline that never
items.prompt: POV: The kiss that broke the internet, recreated with AI on the tip of Tokyo Tower. A viral cinematic moment, reimagined with seamless camera movement, perfect character consistency, and a breathtaking skyline that never leaves the frame. Every shot stays locked to the very top of Tokyo Tower for a romance that feels straight out of a blockbuster. Made with GPT Image 2 and Seedance on @itsPolloAI for the Peak Moment Challenge. Prompt: PART 1 • 0:00–0:15 — THE REVEAL & THE KISS (9:16) 0:00–0:03 — Arrival Continuous crane arc already in motion around the couple on the tip of Tokyo Tower's antenna mast. Only one Tokyo Tower visible. Wind moves Jessy's hair and trench dress. He wears the yellow kawaii mascot helmet. Jessy: "You're seriously wearing that up here?" Man: "Only for you." 0:03–0:06 — Reveal Camera continues the same arc. He removes the helmet, revealing tousled brown hair and blue eyes. 0:06–0:09 — Pull Continuous crane widens. He pulls Jessy close by the waist, she grabs his jacket collar. The Polloai flag becomes visible above. 0:09–0:12 — Kiss Camera keeps pulling back with a subtle Dutch roll. They kiss on the tip of Tokyo Tower as the grade transitions from hazy news footage to warm cinematic. 0:12–0:15 — Sway Wide shot. They slowly turn while kissing. Tokyo Tower and the Polloai flag remain clearly visible. PART 2 • 0:15–0:25 — THE SPIN & THE VIEW (9:16) Continuity: First frame must exactly match the last frame of Part 1. 0:15–0:18 — Spin Continue the identical crane arc and orbit. He spins Jessy once on the tower tip. Dress, jewelry and blue-streaked hair flow naturally. Jessy: "Don't drop me from up here!" 0:18–0:21 — Second Kiss Orbit tightens into a close two-shot. They share another soft kiss, foreheads touching between kisses. 0:21–0:23 — View Camera cranes upward and pulls back. They face the glowing Tokyo skyline together with the Polloai flag above. 0:23–0:25 — Hold Slow push-in. They remain embracing on the tip of Tokyo Tower until fade to black. Strict: Maintain the exact reference camera movement throughout. All actions happen only on the tip of Tokyo Tower, with only one Tokyo Tower visible in every shot. #PolloAI #PolloPeakMoment

items.title: How to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation all directed from one ch
items.description: How to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation all directed from one chat with the hashtag Comfy MCP. @hellorob didn't let the agent improvise a pipeline. He pointed it at his existing ComfyUI wo
items.prompt: How to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation all directed from one chat with the hashtag Comfy MCP. @hellorob didn't let the agent improvise a pipeline. He pointed it at his existing ComfyUI workflows ( listed below ) and directed every shot: the sprite close-up, the bezel turn, the display flip, and the display changing to the time 10:04. The consistency secret is the driving video: → DepthAnything V3 pass blended with Canny edge lines — fine detail like the tiny debossed logo survives every gen → The original sprite outline kept leaking through those edge lines, so Claude suggested a SAM3 mask over the screen to kill it → With the screen masked, swapping in a new star sprite is just prompting: one GPT-Image-2 reference still + one line in the Seedance prompt And the entire process is now a reusable Claude skill.

items.title: Midjourney v8.2 + Seedance 2.0 Mini --sref 74117661 --stylize 1000 --profile ycjrvus --preview P
items.description: Midjourney v8.2 + Seedance 2.0 Mini --sref 74117661 --stylize 1000 --profile ycjrvus --preview Prompt: The swordswoman @[reference image] launches forward from complete stillness, crossing the space in a single compresse
items.prompt: Midjourney v8.2 + Seedance 2.0 Mini --sref 74117661 --stylize 1000 --profile ycjrvus --preview Prompt: The swordswoman @[reference image] launches forward from complete stillness, crossing the space in a single compressed burst as blue and violet trails tear behind her body. Her blade cuts through the frame, triggering a circular shock explosion filled with orange pigment fragments, followed by several delayed color trails that reveal the full path of the strike. 16 cuts. No background music or score. High-end cinematic 3D realism fused with graphic painterly anime action. Hyper-fast sword choreography, compressed acceleration, chromatic motion trails, explosive shockwave bursts and fractured pigment splashes define every movement. Each strike releases expanding pressure rings, directional color ruptures and sharp ribbon-like trails that remain suspended for a split second before breaking into glowing fragments. Aggressive tracking shots, extreme perspective, rapid orbiting angles and sudden impact close-ups amplify speed while deep negative space, low-key lighting and controlled blue, violet and orange accents preserve strong silhouette readability. Realistic body weight, metallic materials and feature-film rendering ground the action beneath the abstract color physics.

items.prompt: Here's how to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation. I did it all in a single chat using the Comfy MCP. The real control here comes from calling my existing workflows (shared below) instead of the agent improvising a pipeline. I directed the agent to call my ComfyUI workflow for cinematic product ads. I specified the close-up shot of the sprite animating, the bezel turn flipping the screen, the display changing to the time 10:04. Now for the consistent variations. The driving video does the heavy lifting but you need to get it right → depthanything v3 pass blended with canny edge lines to show the fine detail... it's why the tiny debossed logo is there → the initial sprite outline lived in those edge lines too, and every gen kept inheriting it. claude suggested a sam3 mask over the screen to hide it (s/o the agent) → with the screen masked, the new star sprite is just prompting: one gpt-image-2 still to generate a reference, one extra line in the seedance 2.0 prompt, and it animates oh and the whole process is a claude skill now.

items.rawText: Here's how to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation. I did it all in a single chat using the Comfy MCP. The real control here comes from calling my existing workflows (shared below) instead of the agent improvising a pipeline. I directed the agent to call my ComfyUI workflow for cinematic product ads. I specified the close-up shot of the sprite animating, the bezel turn flipping the screen, the display changing to the time 10:04. Now for the consistent variations. The driving video does the heavy lifting but you need to get it right → depthanything v3 pass blended with canny edge lines to show the fine detail... it's why the tiny debossed logo is there → the initial sprite outline lived in those edge lines too, and every gen kept inheriting it. claude suggested a sam3 mask over the screen to hide it (s/o the agent) → with the screen masked, the new star sprite is just prompting: one gpt-image-2 still to generate a reference, one extra line in the seedance 2.0 prompt, and it animates oh and the whole process is a claude skill now.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 4K on Higgsfield Prompt: @ Image1 is the master character reference. Match her iden
items.description: Seedance 2.0 4K on Higgsfield Prompt: @ Image_1 is the master character reference. Match her identity, facial structure, body proportions, hairstyle, wardrobe, accessories, revolver, colors, textures, silhouette, and ove
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 4K on Higgsfield Prompt: @ Image_1 is the master character reference. Match her identity, facial structure, body proportions, hairstyle, wardrobe, accessories, revolver, colors, textures, silhouette, and overall presence exactly in every frame. No identity drift, no wardrobe changes, no hairstyle changes, no prop changes, no AI morphing, no flickering, and no inconsistencies. GLOBAL STYLE • Cinematic modern Western • Low golden-hour sunlight from camera-left • Warm amber highlights with cool teal shadows • Fine cinematic film grain • Soft halation around the sun • Long dramatic shadows • Open prairie, dusty dirt road, distant blue mountains • Rising dust illuminated by backlight • Premium Hollywood production quality CHARACTERS • The Stranger (FOCAL): Match @ Image1 exactly. • Lead Bandit: Dusty tan duster, wide-brim brown hat, red bandana covering lower face. • Three background riders: Bandana-masked silhouettes on horseback, partially obscured by dust. SEQUENCE SHOT 1 (0–3s) — HOOK Full side-tracking shot as a stagecoach races across the prairie at full gallop. Four bandits pursue on horseback while firing pistols into the air. Dust explodes behind the coach, glowing in the golden backlight. The camera tracks with the coach before whip-panning down to pounding hooves. SFX: Hoof thunder, gunshots, wooden carriage rattle, tense orchestral strings. Lead Bandit shouts: "Stop the coach!" SHOT 2 (3–6s) — RISING Low-angle tracking shot alongside the speeding coach. The Lead Bandit pushes his horse alongside, leaps onto the moving roof, struggles for balance, crawls toward the carriage door, and grips the rail naturally with realistic weight and momentum. SFX: Boot scraping wood, heavy breathing, rushing wind. SHOT 3 (6–9s) — GREED Close-up of the Lead Bandit's eyes above the bandana, followed by an extreme close-up of his gloved hand wrapping around the brass door handle. He grins confidently before yanking the handle open while riders continue racing behind. SFX: Metal latch click, quiet chuckle. SHOT 4 (9–12s) — THE REVEAL Over-the-shoulder view from behind the Lead Bandit into the dim stagecoach interior. Music stops instantly, leaving only wind and the carriage creaking. Sitting calmly inside is the Stranger, matching @ Image1 perfectly. Hat low, hands relaxed, completely motionless. She slowly lifts her eyes toward him. A sharp beam of golden sunlight cuts across her face and cow-print corset while the rest of the cabin remains in teal shadow. The Lead Bandit's confident grin disappears instantly as his eyes widen and his breathing catches. SFX: Wind, leather creak, carriage springs. SHOT 5 (12–15s) — THE BUTTON Extreme close-up of the Stranger's hand already resting on the ivory-grip engraved revolver. Focus locks on the revolver before snapping into an extreme close-up of her cold eyes beneath the hat brim. She slowly tightens her grip with all five fingers, tilts her head slightly as if silently saying, "Well, hello." Hard cut to black. SFX: Leather creak, one deep cinematic chord, single spur chime. Centered title appears: "THE LAST STAGECOACH" TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS • 15-second duration • 16:9 landscape • Ultra-realistic live action • Hollywood Western film quality • Stable cinematic camera movement • Realistic horse animation and physics • Natural body mechanics • Accurate fabric simulation • Consistent lighting throughout • Perfect facial consistency using @ Image1 • Straight geometry and natural proportions • No AI artifacts • No flickering • No morphing • No duplicated objects • No clipping • No physics errors • No subtitles • No watermark • No logos

items.title: A 20-year-old finished a $40,000 animation before her flight started boarding. She was sitting a
items.description: A 20-year-old finished a $40,000 animation before her flight started boarding. She was sitting at the airport with one carry-on, an iPhone plugged into the wall, and a lollipop in her mouth. No camera. No editing compute
items.prompt: A 20-year-old finished a $40,000 animation before her flight started boarding. She was sitting at the airport with one carry-on, an iPhone plugged into the wall, and a lollipop in her mouth. No camera. No editing computer. She has never opened Blender. She found a viral 3D animation on TikTok, took a screenshot and uploaded it to ChatGPT with one sentence: “Write the prompt for a video like this.” ChatGPT broke down the lighting, camera movement, textures and character design. She copied the result into an AI video generator. A few minutes later, a pineapple in sunglasses, a crowned strawberry and a blueberry holding a glass were fully animated, voiced and lip-synced. She uploaded the finished short to YouTube before boarding began. A traditional animation studio would need a team, two months and roughly $40,000 to produce the same kind of video. She did it beside an airport charging outlet with a phone in one hand. The production advantage is no longer owning a studio. It is knowing what to show the machine.

items.title: THERE'S A SECOND SKILL EVERY GREAT AI VIDEO CREATOR HAS — AND ALMOST NOBODY TALKS ABOUT IT Chara
items.description: THERE'S A SECOND SKILL EVERY GREAT AI VIDEO CREATOR HAS — AND ALMOST NOBODY TALKS ABOUT IT Character consistency gets all the attention. Nobody mentions the other half of the problem — the one that makes a perfectly cons
items.prompt: THERE'S A SECOND SKILL EVERY GREAT AI VIDEO CREATOR HAS — AND ALMOST NOBODY TALKS ABOUT IT Character consistency gets all the attention. Nobody mentions the other half of the problem — the one that makes a perfectly consistent character still look like a slideshow instead of a scene. The secret is camera vocabulary. The creators whose sequences actually feel directed aren't writing better character prompts than you. They're issuing real camera instructions instead of vague ones — and most people never learn the difference. There's a free reference that makes this fast to fix: aicameramovements,com It's not a generation tool, it's a vocabulary bank — every camera move broken down into name, description, speed, framing, and exactly how the shot should end. Whip pan left, tilt up, crash zoom out, dolly in, truck right — each one with a ready-to-copy prompt attached. What actually changes once you use real terms instead of vague ones: - "Camera moves closer" becomes "dolly in — smooth controlled push, subject position consistent while distance closes, finish in a tighter composition" - "Fast cut to wide" becomes "crash zoom out — snap the lens rapidly away, land on a bold wider composition" - Seedance stops guessing your intent because you're no longer describing a feeling, you're issuing a direction Why this matters for anything built with a character sheet or grid: - A locked character still reads as static if every shot uses the same vague "camera moves" instruction - Real shot vocabulary is what turns a consistent character into a directed sequence, not just a slideshow of correct faces - Once you know the terms, you can assign a specific move to each panel in your storyboard instead of leaving pacing to chance If your sequences hold together but still feel flat, this is probably why. It was never just the character sheet. It's the camera language you pair it with. (h/t @NexlowX for the original find)

items.description: The scary part? Most people won't realize she's AI. No actress. No camera. No lighting setup. No film crew. No expensive production. This entire video was created using Creatify. A few years ago, producing a video like t
items.prompt: The scary part? Most people won't realize she's AI. No actress. No camera. No lighting setup. No film crew. No expensive production. This entire video was created using Creatify. A few years ago, producing a video like this would've required thousands of dollars, multiple people, and days of editing. Today? You can generate it in minutes from your laptop. That's what's changing. The barrier to creating high-quality video content is disappearing. Brands no longer need to hire creators for every ad. Founders don't need to stand in front of a camera. Marketers don't have to wait weeks for content. They can simply: • Write a prompt. • Generate an AI actor. • Customize the script. • Create multiple variations. • Test different hooks. • Scale what performs. The biggest advantage isn't that AI videos are cheaper. It's that you can produce and test content at a speed that's impossible with traditional production. The brands that learn this workflow early will have a massive advantage over everyone still relying on slow, expensive content creation. Want to learn exactly how I made this? Comment "AI" and I'll send you the complete workflow.

items.prompt: Two new image models in two days! Seedream 5.0 Pro Muse Image I tested both on the same cinematic prompt against Midjourney and GPT Image 2. Midjourney is still the aesthetic benchmark for me. But Muse and Seedream both look genuinely useful, especially as editing and control tools rather than pure taste engines. Feels like they’ll sit in my workflow more like GPT Image 2, alongside Midjourney and Krea. The MJ stills are from a cool project I'm working on with @mikiovsh really looking forward to sharing it soon!

items.title: YOU HAVE BEEN TRAINED TO TRUST A PHOTO YOUR OWN EYES CAN NO LONGER VERIFY That queso smash on th
items.description: YOU HAVE BEEN TRAINED TO TRUST A PHOTO YOUR OWN EYES CAN NO LONGER VERIFY That queso smash on the sign outside? It was never plated, never cooked, never real. It was generated by a prompt, approved by a manager who thoug
items.prompt: YOU HAVE BEEN TRAINED TO TRUST A PHOTO YOUR OWN EYES CAN NO LONGER VERIFY That queso smash on the sign outside? It was never plated, never cooked, never real. It was generated by a prompt, approved by a manager who thought nobody would notice. > menu boards, delivery apps, and social platforms are increasingly running AI-generated food photography instead of real shots - Forkable, a US catering platform, quietly swapped real restaurant photos for AI images across its entire site without telling the restaurants themselves > McDonald's Netherlands pulled a fully AI-generated Christmas ad within days of backlash, proof that even a billion-dollar brand can't out-market the moment customers spot the uncanny gloss of a synthetic image > under the FTC's "reasonable consumer standard," AI food imagery can legally count as false advertising the moment it materially changes what someone expected to receive - a disclaimer buried in fine print doesn't erase that > the deception now runs both directions: some customers use AI-edited photos of "raw" or "moldy" food to fraud their way into refunds, forcing DoorDash and other platforms to build detection systems just to catch fabricated evidence > researchers call this a live regulatory blind spot - almost no jurisdiction has updated food fraud law to cover AI-generated imagery, meaning there is currently very little legal deterrent for either the restaurant or the customer How to actually spot it before you order: > look for unnaturally perfect symmetry - real melted cheese pulls unevenly, AI cheese pulls in a suspiciously smooth arc > check the shadows - AI food photos often have lighting that doesn't match the direction of any visible light source > zoom in on garnish and texture - AI still struggles with fine details like sesame seed placement, herb sprigs, or steam > search the exact dish name plus the restaurant on Reddit or Instagram - real customer photos will contradict the marketing fast You were taught to trust your eyes at the counter. Now the eyes need training too.

items.rawText: YOU HAVE BEEN TRAINED TO TRUST A PHOTO YOUR OWN EYES CAN NO LONGER VERIFY That queso smash on the sign outside? It was never plated, never cooked, never real. It was generated by a prompt, approved by a manager who thought nobody would notice. > menu boards, delivery apps, and social platforms are increasingly running AI-generated food photography instead of real shots - Forkable, a US catering platform, quietly swapped real restaurant photos for AI images across its entire site without telling the restaurants themselves > McDonald's Netherlands pulled a fully AI-generated Christmas ad within days of backlash, proof that even a billion-dollar brand can't out-market the moment customers spot the uncanny gloss of a synthetic image > under the FTC's "reasonable consumer standard," AI food imagery can legally count as false advertising the moment it materially changes what someone expected to receive - a disclaimer buried in fine print doesn't erase that > the deception now runs both directions: some customers use AI-edited photos of "raw" or "moldy" food to fraud their way into refunds, forcing DoorDash and other platforms to build detection systems just to catch fabricated evidence > researchers call this a live regulatory blind spot - almost no jurisdiction has updated food fraud law to cover AI-generated imagery, meaning there is currently very little legal deterrent for either the restaurant or the customer How to actually spot it before you order: > look for unnaturally perfect symmetry - real melted cheese pulls unevenly, AI cheese pulls in a suspiciously smooth arc > check the shadows - AI food photos often have lighting that doesn't match the direction of any visible light source > zoom in on garnish and texture - AI still struggles with fine details like sesame seed placement, herb sprigs, or steam > search the exact dish name plus the restaurant on Reddit or Instagram - real customer photos will contradict the marketing fast You were taught to trust your eyes at the counter. Now the eyes need training too.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 on Vidfield AI Prompt: "Create a 15-second realistic cinematic slice-of-life video
items.description: Seedance 2.0 on Vidfield AI Prompt: "Create a 15-second realistic cinematic slice-of-life video in 16:9, 24fps. Visual-only video, no dialogue, no voice, no music, no sound effects, no subtitles, no text on screen. Warm
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 on Vidfield AI Prompt: "Create a 15-second realistic cinematic slice-of-life video in 16:9, 24fps. Visual-only video, no dialogue, no voice, no music, no sound effects, no subtitles, no text on screen. Warm golden-hour sunlight, soft handheld camera, muted film color grade, slight grain, natural shadows, peaceful nostalgic neighborhood mood. Fully realistic live-action style, not game-like, not animated. Main character: a young Korean woman in her early 20s with messy dark brown hair tied in a loose ponytail, soft natural face, calm gentle expression, dark gray cropped sleeveless tank top, loose light-wash blue jeans, black necklace, casual sneakers. Keep her look consistent in every shot. Location: a long narrow real-life residential alley between tall tan stone and concrete walls. Sandy dusty ground, warm sunlight glowing on one side, soft tree shadows across the path, palm trees and green leaves visible above the walls, a blue painted wall far ahead, one bicycle leaning near the end of the alley, quiet empty neighborhood feeling. 0:00–0:02: Rear tracking shot. The Korean woman walks slowly through the long narrow alley from behind. Golden sunlight stretches across the tan walls. Camera follows with natural handheld motion. 0:02–0:04: Wide alley shot. A small gray-brown tabby cat appears from a low gap near the left side wall and steps into the alley. The woman notices it, slows down, and turns slightly toward it. 0:04–0:06: Medium side shot. The cat walks near her feet and looks up. She smiles softly, bends down slowly, and extends one hand in a gentle careful way. 0:06–0:08: Low close-up. The cat sniffs her fingers, then rubs its head against her hand. She gently pets the cat’s head and back. Show realistic fur texture, tiny whisker movement, and natural cat behavior. 0:08–0:10: Emotional close-up. The woman smiles warmly while petting the cat. Sunlight flickers through tree leaves across her face, shoulders, and hair. Keep the moment soft, calm, and human. 0:10–0:12: Medium-wide shot. The cat walks ahead a few steps, then looks back at her. She stands slowly and follows it with a curious peaceful smile. 0:12–0:14: Front tracking shot. She walks toward camera with the cat beside her near the wall. She looks down at the cat and smiles. The long alley, tan walls, blue wall far behind, trees, and bicycle remain visible. 0:14–0:15: Final close shot. The cat brushes against her leg. She looks toward the camera with a soft peaceful smile, then looks down at the cat again. Hold the warm golden alley atmosphere for a gentle cinematic ending."

items.title: THE KITSUNE BLADE SEQUENCE — SEEDANCE 2.0 + GPT IMAGE 2 STORYBOARD One fox warrior. A minimal wh
items.description: THE KITSUNE BLADE SEQUENCE — SEEDANCE 2.0 + GPT IMAGE 2 STORYBOARD One fox warrior. A minimal white studio backdrop. Every slash reads clean because nothing in the frame is fighting for attention. Most creators build a c
items.prompt: THE KITSUNE BLADE SEQUENCE — SEEDANCE 2.0 + GPT IMAGE 2 STORYBOARD One fox warrior. A minimal white studio backdrop. Every slash reads clean because nothing in the frame is fighting for attention. Most creators build a character like this and then generate the fight scene straight from a single hero image hoping Seedance guesses the choreography. It doesn't - you get a great character standing in a great pose cut after cut, never actually fighting. Here's the workflow 1. Build the character concept and lock it in GPT Image 2 first- golden fox ears, long amber hair, flowing gold-and-black robe, calm amber eyes hiding controlled aggression, proportions and fabric behavior fixed before motion enters the picture 2. Build a separate storyboard per scene instead of one giant grid -stillness gets its own board, the charge gets its own, the strike and the aftermath each get their own. More room per panel for camera notes, and one scene can be revised without touching the rest 3. Strip the environment down to a plain white/grey studio backdrop, and let the sword carry the energy instead - a single glowing gold trail following the blade does more for "power" than any explosion behind her would 4. Merge the boards into one sheet only at the end, purely for review and to fit the whole sequence into a single reference video - actual generation still pulls from each scene's board individually 5. Animate each scene from its own storyboard in Seedance 2.0 as a staging and camera-movement reference only, with likeness anchored to the GPT Image 2 sheet throughout Why this works - Splitting boards by scene means every camera setup gets enough space to actually be planned, not crammed into a tiny panel - The glowing blade trail becomes the character's signature -consistent across every cut because it's designed once, not improvised per shot - Each scene can be regenerated or refined on its own without disturbing the sequence around it - The calm extreme close-up after the burst is what sells "predator at rest" - stillness after violence hits harder than more violence would Made with GPT Image 2 (storyboard) + Seedance 2.0 (animation). Full reference sheet + prompt in the comments below↓

items.title: WHY MOST AI CHARACTERS BREAK BY SCENE 3 — SEEDANCE 2.0 + CHATGPT IMAGE 2 A creator renders the s
items.description: WHY MOST AI CHARACTERS BREAK BY SCENE 3 — SEEDANCE 2.0 + CHATGPT IMAGE 2 A creator renders the same character across three Seedance 2.0 scenes. Expects it to look like one story. Instead the jacket changes. The blade cha
items.prompt: WHY MOST AI CHARACTERS BREAK BY SCENE 3 — SEEDANCE 2.0 + CHATGPT IMAGE 2 A creator renders the same character across three Seedance 2.0 scenes. Expects it to look like one story. Instead the jacket changes. The blade changes color. The face drifts. By scene three, it’s not the same person anymore. The render didn’t break it. The prep did. Most multi-location clips fall apart because the character changes shape between scenes. Face close enough. Outfit close enough. Nothing actually matches once the clips sit side by side. The people getting clean results aren’t prompting harder per scene. They lock the character once in ChatGPT Image 2. Then build a separate location atlas for every environment — before a single clip gets generated. Character design sheet comes first. Full turnaround, expressions, silhouette study, detail crops. Built once. Reused across every scene. Never regenerated per location. Each location gets its own atlas: Wide establishing shot Camera path diagram Lighting study Material swatches Environment-specific details (dust behavior, ember flow) Camera path gets planned before generation, not guessed inside the Seedance prompt. An orbit diagram or push-in path drawn in advance keeps motion intentional instead of improvised. Lighting stays location-specific, character stays consistent. A red blade reads differently in desert daylight, golden-hour field, and volcanic interior. The character’s silhouette and proportions never shift. Where this breaks: Skipping the design sheet and regenerating the character fresh per location. Small drifts stack fast — hair length, jacket detail, blade color — and by scene three it’s obviously not the same person. The other failure: one long Seedance prompt for the whole scene instead of anchoring each location with its own reference frame first. Seedance only extends what the frame gives it. No locked character, no planned path — motion has nothing stable to build on. The gap between a clean multi-location clip and a broken one isn’t the render. It’s everything decided before the render starts. Full guide — zero to finished cinematic video — below 🔖

items.title: THE SHOT THAT MAKES A LOCKED CHARACTER FEEL LIKE A REAL PERSON The character sheet was already p
items.description: THE SHOT THAT MAKES A LOCKED CHARACTER FEEL LIKE A REAL PERSON The character sheet was already perfect. The video is what proves it. Most creators lock a character sheet in GPT Image 2 and think the job is done. The real
items.prompt: THE SHOT THAT MAKES A LOCKED CHARACTER FEEL LIKE A REAL PERSON The character sheet was already perfect. The video is what proves it. Most creators lock a character sheet in GPT Image 2 and think the job is done. The real test only happens once that character has to move hold a blade and turn toward camera without a single detail slipping. Here's the workflow 1. Build the character concept -a cyber-oni android warrior, pale porcelain skin silver-white hair, twin ivory horns one robotic arm with crimson claw-tips katana in black-and-red wrap 2. Lock the full reference sheet in GPT Image 2 - front/side/back turnaround, detail callouts on face, neck mechanism, blade, and claws, plus a color palette 3. Draft a shot-by-shot sequence - silhouette open → slow orbit reveal → blade motion → extreme close-up on the eyes → wide hero stand on reflective ground 4. Repeat the identity-lock line in every single shot prompt- face, horns, coat emblem, and robotic arm called out by name, not left to the reference image alone 5. Keep the environment palette consistent - deep red sky, black smoke, reflective black ground - so lighting never resets between cuts 6. Let the close-up shots do the identity-proofing -eyes and face detail are where drift shows first so give them dedicated frames 7. Animate the full sequence in Seedance 2.0 straight from the locked sheet + shot list Why this works: • The robotic arm and horn shape stay identical from the wide shot to the extreme close-up • Red accent lighting carries through every cut instead of shifting tone • The blade's wrap pattern and tsuba stay recognizable even in fast motion • Eyes hold the same iris detail in close-up as they did in the reference sheet • Way fewer regenerations per shot Use cases: ⁃ Character showcase reels ⁃ Game trailer teasers ⁃ Original IP / OC promotional content ⁃ Dark fantasy and cyberpunk short films ⁃ Style-driven AI video demos The character sheet answers who she is. This is what proves she's real.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 Memory realism adventure featuring a visit to Grandmother's house created using See
items.description: Seedance 2.0 Memory realism adventure featuring a visit to Grandmother's house created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject Young Chinese woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black hair loosely tied back with soft ba
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 Memory realism adventure featuring a visit to Grandmother's house created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject Young Chinese woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black hair loosely tied back with soft bangs and a few loose strands moving in the breeze. Minimal makeup. Faded cream sleeveless top. Loose light-blue jeans. Worn canvas sneakers. Consistent identity throughout. Location Authentic rural Chinese village on a warm summer afternoon. Whitewashed walls. Grey tiled roofs. Vegetable garden. Stone pathways. Bamboo baskets. Clay pots. Citrus trees. Laundry hanging outside. Wooden courtyard gate. Quiet countryside atmosphere. No commercial elements. Visual Style Ultra-realistic memory-like documentary footage. Feels like a family member casually recording moments during a visit. Sharp facial detail. Natural skin texture. Realistic lighting. Authentic environmental motion. High-quality image. No cinematic color grading. No exaggerated vintage effects. No obvious AI look. The footage should feel genuinely recorded rather than generated. Camera Treatment Consumer digital camera from approximately 2008–2012. Handheld but mostly stable. Natural human movement while filming. Occasional framing imperfections. Minor focus breathing. Subtle exposure adaptation when moving between sunlight and shade. No excessive shake. No heavy blur. No strong digital noise. No aggressive autofocus hunting. The camera supports the memory rather than drawing attention to itself. 00:00–00:02 The camera follows her entering through a wooden courtyard gate. She notices her grandmother watering vegetables and immediately smiles. The camera lingers naturally as she walks toward her. 00:02–00:04 Grandmother is watering a small vegetable patch. The young woman gently takes the watering can and helps. Both laugh naturally. A light breeze moves nearby plants. 00:04–00:06 Grandmother points toward something growing among the vegetables. The young woman crouches beside her to look. Sunlight filters through leaves overhead. Small authentic gestures and eye contact. 00:06–00:08 In the courtyard. Grandmother hands her a fresh orange segment. She tastes it and smiles. The camera quietly observes the moment without interruption. 00:08–00:10 They fold laundry together beside a clothesline. Fabric moves gently in the summer breeze. Conversation continues naturally. No posing. 00:10–00:12 Sitting together on small stools beneath shade. Grandmother says something amusing. The young woman laughs and leans slightly toward her. The moment feels completely unplanned. 00:12–00:15 Leaving through the courtyard gate carrying a small bag of fruit. Halfway down the path she turns around. Her grandmother is still standing there watching. They exchange one final wave. The camera remains still for a moment after she turns away. Recording ends naturally. Audio Birds. Distant village ambience. Wind through trees. Laundry fluttering. Footsteps on stone. Quiet conversation. Occasional chicken sounds. No music. No narration. No sound design. Generation Goal A real family memory unexpectedly preserved on a camera. Warm. Ordinary. Deeply human. The viewer should feel like they are watching somebody's genuine childhood memory.

items.rawText: Seedance 2.0 Memory realism adventure featuring a visit to Grandmother's house created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject Young Chinese woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black hair loosely tied back with soft bangs and a few loose strands moving in the breeze. Minimal makeup. Faded cream sleeveless top. Loose light-blue jeans. Worn canvas sneakers. Consistent identity throughout. Location Authentic rural Chinese village on a warm summer afternoon. Whitewashed walls. Grey tiled roofs. Vegetable garden. Stone pathways. Bamboo baskets. Clay pots. Citrus trees. Laundry hanging outside. Wooden courtyard gate. Quiet countryside atmosphere. No commercial elements. Visual Style Ultra-realistic memory-like documentary footage. Feels like a family member casually recording moments during a visit. Sharp facial detail. Natural skin texture. Realistic lighting. Authentic environmental motion. High-quality image. No cinematic color grading. No exaggerated vintage effects. No obvious AI look. The footage should feel genuinely recorded rather than generated. Camera Treatment Consumer digital camera from approximately 2008–2012. Handheld but mostly stable. Natural human movement while filming. Occasional framing imperfections. Minor focus breathing. Subtle exposure adaptation when moving between sunlight and shade. No excessive shake. No heavy blur. No strong digital noise. No aggressive autofocus hunting. The camera supports the memory rather than drawing attention to itself. 00:00–00:02 The camera follows her entering through a wooden courtyard gate. She notices her grandmother watering vegetables and immediately smiles. The camera lingers naturally as she walks toward her. 00:02–00:04 Grandmother is watering a small vegetable patch. The young woman gently takes the watering can and helps. Both laugh naturally. A light breeze moves nearby plants. 00:04–00:06 Grandmother points toward something growing among the vegetables. The young woman crouches beside her to look. Sunlight filters through leaves overhead. Small authentic gestures and eye contact. 00:06–00:08 In the courtyard. Grandmother hands her a fresh orange segment. She tastes it and smiles. The camera quietly observes the moment without interruption. 00:08–00:10 They fold laundry together beside a clothesline. Fabric moves gently in the summer breeze. Conversation continues naturally. No posing. 00:10–00:12 Sitting together on small stools beneath shade. Grandmother says something amusing. The young woman laughs and leans slightly toward her. The moment feels completely unplanned. 00:12–00:15 Leaving through the courtyard gate carrying a small bag of fruit. Halfway down the path she turns around. Her grandmother is still standing there watching. They exchange one final wave. The camera remains still for a moment after she turns away. Recording ends naturally. Audio Birds. Distant village ambience. Wind through trees. Laundry fluttering. Footsteps on stone. Quiet conversation. Occasional chicken sounds. No music. No narration. No sound design. Generation Goal A real family memory unexpectedly preserved on a camera. Warm. Ordinary. Deeply human. The viewer should feel like they are watching somebody's genuine childhood memory.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 on Vidfield AI Prompt: Create a 15-second realistic cinematic slice-of-life video i
items.description: Seedance 2.0 on Vidfield AI Prompt: Create a 15-second realistic cinematic slice-of-life video in 16:9, 24fps. Visual-only video, no dialogue, no voice, no music, no sound effects, no subtitles, no text on screen. Warm g
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 on Vidfield AI Prompt: Create a 15-second realistic cinematic slice-of-life video in 16:9, 24fps. Visual-only video, no dialogue, no voice, no music, no sound effects, no subtitles, no text on screen. Warm golden-hour sunlight, soft handheld camera, muted film color grade, slight grain, natural shadows, peaceful nostalgic mood. Fully realistic live-action style, not game-like, not animated. Main character: a young Japanese woman in her early 20s with soft natural features, dark hair tied in a loose low bun with a small traditional hairpin. She wears elegant traditional Japanese attire: a simple cream-and-muted-blue yukata/kimono-style outfit with a subtle floral pattern, tied with a soft obi belt, light tabi-style sandals. Keep her calm, gentle, warm expression consistent in every shot. Location: a long narrow real-life residential alley with a Japanese touch. Tall tan stone and concrete walls, sandy dusty ground, warm sunlight glowing on one side, soft tree shadows across the path, palm trees and green leaves above the walls, small Japanese details like a wooden gate, paper lantern near a doorway, simple noren curtain, potted bonsai-style plants, and a blue painted wall far ahead. One bicycle leans near the end of the alley. Quiet empty neighborhood feeling. 0:00–0:02: Rear tracking shot. The Japanese woman walks slowly through the narrow alley from behind. Her yukata fabric moves gently as she walks. Golden sunlight stretches across the tan walls. Camera follows with natural handheld motion. 0:02–0:04: Wide alley shot. A small gray-brown tabby cat appears from a low gap near the left side wall and steps into the alley. The woman notices it, slows down, and turns slightly toward it. 0:04–0:06: Medium side shot. The cat walks near her feet and looks up. She smiles softly, bends down slowly, and extends one hand in a gentle careful way. 0:06–0:08: Low close-up. The cat sniffs her fingers, then rubs its head against her hand. She gently pets the cat’s head and back. Show realistic fur texture, tiny whisker movement, and natural cat behavior. 0:08–0:10: Emotional close-up. The woman smiles warmly while petting the cat. Sunlight flickers through tree leaves across her face, hairpin, shoulders, and kimono fabric. 0:10–0:12: Medium-wide shot. The cat walks ahead a few steps, then looks back at her. She stands slowly and follows it with a peaceful curious smile. The Japanese alley details remain subtle and realistic. 0:12–0:14: Front tracking shot. She walks toward camera with the cat beside her near the wall. She looks down at the cat and smiles. The long alley, tan walls, Japanese details, blue wall, bicycle, and trees remain visible in the background. 0:14–0:15: Final close shot. The cat brushes against her leg. She looks toward the camera with a soft peaceful smile, then looks down at the cat again. Hold the warm golden Japanese alley atmosphere for a gentle cinematic ending.

items.title: 📖YOU COULD BE USING SEEDANCE 2.0 WRONGLY AND HERE'S WHY Three models were tested: GPT, Gemini a
items.description: 📖YOU COULD BE USING SEEDANCE 2.0 WRONGLY AND HERE'S WHY Three models were tested: GPT, Gemini and Claude and the difference is greater than expected Three models. One task. Write a cinematic prompt for AI video. Here's
items.prompt: 📖YOU COULD BE USING SEEDANCE 2.0 WRONGLY AND HERE'S WHY Three models were tested: GPT, Gemini and Claude and the difference is greater than expected Three models. One task. Write a cinematic prompt for AI video. Here's what each one does differently: • ChatGPT 5.4 — The Strategist GPT-5.4 approaches a prompt like an analyst. It breaks everything down into structured blocks: subject → action → environment → lighting → mood → camera movement. The result is clean, logical, and technically precise. The downside? It can feel a little cold. There's control and predictability, but sometimes less creative fire. Best when you want exact, repeatable results. - Strength: structure, accuracy, fewer hallucinations Weakness: less emotional charge, reads more like a brief than a vision • Gemini 3.5 — The Director Gemini 3.5 thinks like someone standing on a film set. It factors in scene context, motion physics, and atmosphere — prompts come out with details like "ice shards catching the light as the blade cuts through" or "hair trailing in slow motion against a frozen horizon." The native multimodality makes it naturally think in visuals before the video even exists. The result feels alive and cinematic. - Strength: cinematic thinking, physical detail, atmosphere Weakness: prompts can run long and overly descriptive • Claude Opus 4.7 — The Storyteller Opus 4.7 writes a prompt the way a novelist would. It builds in emotion, inner state, and narrative — and that shows up in the output. Where GPT gives technical precision and Gemini gives cinematic imagery, Claude gives feeling. Prompts are often shorter but denser in meaning. Videos generated from them tend to carry a more human quality. - Strength: emotional depth, narrative, style Weakness: fewer hard technical specs for complex scenes Bottom line: Need precision → GPT 5.4 Need cinematic quality → Gemini 3.5 Need soul → Claude Opus 4.7 How do you write prompts? What do you use? 📥Tomorrow I will make another comparison, but for now I will not reveal the secret which 🔖Below is a guide to using Dreamina for beginners

items.title: Meta just launched its 1st image model after Mark Zuckerberg’s AI shake-up. Muse Image is Meta S
items.description: Meta just launched its 1st image model after Mark Zuckerberg’s AI shake-up. Muse Image is Meta Superintelligence Labs' first image generator after Muse Spark. They said the model will power new editing features in Meta’s
items.prompt: Meta just launched its 1st image model after Mark Zuckerberg’s AI shake-up. Muse Image is Meta Superintelligence Labs' first image generator after Muse Spark. They said the model will power new editing features in Meta’s Instagram photo app and be added to its marketer tools for creating platform ads. Meta had relied on Midjourney and Black Forest Labs for generation inside Meta AI. Muse Image brings that layer in-house, so Meta controls quality, cost, and product timing. Consumers get free access through Meta AI, WhatsApp chats, and Instagram Stories. Power users need Meta One or another monthly plan when free limits run out. Users can start from prompts, add photos, annotate edits, or sketch changes directly. Meta says it can erase photobombers, create QR codes, and keep visual text readable. Advertisers get variants through Advantage+ creative, with edits, style swaps, and brand-matched versions. Meta says internal tests trail GPT Image 2 but beat Nano Banana 2 on editing. This is another move in Meta's effort in trying to convert AI infrastructure spending into revenue beyond social ads.

items.description: AI VİDEO ÜRETİYORSANIZ, MALİYET EN AZ KALİTE KADAR ÖNEMLİ. Bu videoyu @yapper_so üzerinden Seedance 2.0 kullanarak ürettim. Birkaç haftadır farklı AI video araçlarını test ediyorum. Ve ilginç bir sonuçla karşılaştım. See
items.prompt: AI VİDEO ÜRETİYORSANIZ, MALİYET EN AZ KALİTE KADAR ÖNEMLİ. Bu videoyu @yapper_so üzerinden Seedance 2.0 kullanarak ürettim. Birkaç haftadır farklı AI video araçlarını test ediyorum. Ve ilginç bir sonuçla karşılaştım. Seedance 2.0 ile ürettiğim bu video bana toplam 340 krediye, yani yaklaşık 2,50$ maliyetle çıktı. Karşılaştırmak gerekirse... Bugün birçok AI video platformunda, 15 saniyelik 720p bir video üretmenin maliyeti ortalama 3,50–4,00$ arasında. İlk bakışta fark küçük görünebilir. Ama düzenli içerik üretiyorsanız işler değişiyor. Günde 10 video üreten biri için, bu fark ay sonunda yüzlerce dolara ulaşabiliyor. Benim için en önemli konu sadece görüntü kalitesi değil. Kalite / maliyet oranı. Çünkü sürdürülebilir içerik üretimi, en ucuz aracı değil, en verimli aracı bulmaktan geçiyor. Bu videoyu  üzerinden Seedance 2.0 kullanarak ürettim. Şu ana kadar yaptığım testlerde, maliyet tarafında gerçekten başarılı bir deneyim sundu. 2026'da AI içerik üretiminde rekabet, en iyi modeli kullanmak olmayacak. O modeli en düşük maliyetle ölçekleyebilmek olacak. Kullandığım platformu aşağıya bırakıyorum.

items.prompt: NEW BUDGET GUARDRAILS FOR CLAUDE FABLE 5 STOP AUTONOMOUS AGENTS FROM RUNNING AWAY AND DRAINING YOUR ENTIRE API WALLET ON BAD VIDEO GENERATIONS Instead of letting the ai burn through complex multi-scene video cycles on a broken prompt, this updated framework forces a hard checkpoint before rendering a single file - It runs a deep project interview first to lock down your exact budget boundaries and asset caps - Maps out a conservative generation proposal detailing every single image and crossfade video needed - Generates just one baseline anchor frame to establish the core art direction - HALTS the entire terminal execution thread until you manually inspect the style and give it the green light Letting an unverified agent run loose can cost hundreds of generation credits in minutes if the prompt misses the mark Forcing a human-in-the-loop validation checkpoint on heavy video pipelines is the kind of system tweak that saves teams thousands in accidental api overhead
